title: Dan Ives Calls SpaceX IPO A 'Goldilocks Outcome' That Signals Green Light For OpenAI, Anthropic Debuts
author: Rishabh Mishra
description: Wedbush analyst Dan Ives characterized SpaceX's IPO debut as a 'Goldilocks outcome' for the tech sector, with the stock opening at $150 (11.1% above the $135 IPO price) and closing 19.3% higher at $160.95. Ives believes the successful SpaceX listing signals investor appetite for capital-intensive ventures and clears the path for OpenAI and Anthropic IPOs before year-end. He maintains that the broader tech and chip sectors have significant upside potential as the AI spending cycle is only in its 'third inning.'

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong IPO debut with 11.1% premium to IPO price and 19.3% first-day gain; characterized as 'Goldilocks outcome' signaling healthy investor appetite and market conditions for tech/AI sector
Highlighted by Wedbush as a 'core winner' in the data orchestration layer as frontier AI models commoditize
Identified as a 'core winner' in data orchestration, positioned to benefit from AI model commoditization trends
Named as a 'core winner' in the data orchestration layer benefiting from AI industry evolution
Highlighted as a 'core winner' in data orchestration as value shifts away from frontier AI models

title: The AI Boom Has a Hidden Winner—And It’s Not Nvidia
author: Thomas Hughes
description: Cybersecurity stocks are emerging as the hidden winners of the AI boom, rebounding strongly with approximately 100% gains from early 2026 lows. Companies like Palo Alto Networks, CrowdStrike, and Datadog have received significant analyst upgrades, with cybersecurity being essential to the AI software stack. Institutional buying has resumed in 2026, and valuations suggest 50-100% upside potential over coming years, with major catalysts expected from Q2 earnings and datacenter buildouts.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as fourth most upgraded stock with 15% upside to $375 consensus price target. Q1 2026 earnings triggered upgrades, and technical charts show strengthening market with likely retests of recent highs.

Ranked first among most upgraded stocks with 56 updates lifting consensus price target by 35% within days. Strong Q1 earnings performance and institutional accumulation in 2026 support continued momentum.

Listed as sixth most upgraded stock with best-case scenario of $300, representing significant upside. Moderate Buy rating with improving coverage and sentiment, benefiting from AI-driven security demand.

Mentioned as the obvious AI winner that the article contrasts against. While not negatively portrayed, the article positions cybersecurity as the overlooked beneficiary rather than highlighting Nvidia's continued strength.

title: Software Stocks Are Sinking In 2026, But These 'Fab Five' Are Up 30% — And BofA Says They're Just Getting Started
author: Piero Cingari
description: Bank of America identifies five enterprise software stocks—Datadog, JFrog, MongoDB, Snowflake, and Twilio—as outperformers in 2026, collectively up 30% while the broader software sector declines 12%. The analyst attributes their strength to solid execution, AI exposure, and differentiated products, with AI-related revenue growing over 100% at Datadog and strong adoption across all five companies. BofA maintains Buy ratings on all five, expecting the gap between these winners and struggling peers to widen.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Surged 58% post-earnings with 32% YoY revenue growth acceleration. AI business growing over 100% annually and representing over 10% of revenue. Maintained Buy rating by BofA analyst.
Advanced 43% post-earnings with cloud revenue growth reaching 50%, up from 42% prior quarter. Increasing adoption among AI-focused customers. Maintained Buy rating.
Rose 5% post-earnings with Atlas revenue growth at 29.4%. Reported increasing adoption among AI-focused customers. BofA remains confident in long-term outlook with Buy rating maintained.
Climbed 37% post-earnings with product revenue growth accelerating to 34% YoY. AI products (Cortex AI and Cortex Code) driving growth. Maintained Buy rating.
Gained 38% post-earnings with gross-profit-dollar growth improving to 16% from 10%. Momentum from voice AI products. Maintained Buy rating.
Down 12% in 2026, representing the broader software sector decline that contrasts sharply with the outperformance of the 'Fab Five' stocks.

title: 3 Most Upgraded Stocks Last Month According to MarketBeat
author: Leo Miller
description: Three technology stocks dominated analyst upgrades in May 2026: Datadog and Marvell Technology tied for most upgraded with 32 upgrades each, while Snowflake received 28 upgrades. All three stocks surged significantly—Datadog up 87.1%, Marvell up 24%, and Snowflake up 87%—driven by strong earnings beats, raised guidance, and momentum in the AI software and hardware trade. However, current price targets suggest potential downside for Datadog and Marvell, though Snowflake shows moderate upside potential.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Received 32 analyst upgrades (tied for most), stock surged 87.1% in May with strong Q1 earnings beat, raised full-year guidance to $4.32B (25% YoY growth), and obtained FedRAMP High certification. 41 Buy ratings vs. 2 Sell ratings indicate strong analyst confidence despite 20% downside implied by price targets.

Received 32 analyst upgrades (tied for most), gained 24% in May following 67% April gain. Q1 FY2027 earnings showed slight sales beat with substantial guidance raises ($500M for FY2027, $1.5B for FY2028). Benefits from AI hardware trade strength. 31 Buy ratings with zero Sells indicate strong analyst support despite implied downside from consensus price target.

Received 28 analyst upgrades, achieved 87% monthly gain (largest ever) driven by Q1 FY2027 earnings beats on sales and EPS. Raised full-year guidance to $5.84B and committed $6 billion to Amazon for Graviton chips, signaling confidence in growth. 36 Buy ratings vs. 1 Sell rating. Price targets imply moderate to healthy upside potential.
Delivered 19.8% total return in May, nearly matching April's 20% gain, driven by strong investor demand for AI-related technology stocks and software companies rebounding after earlier weakness.

title: Wall Street Dumped This Magnificent ETF, but It's Making a Roaring Comeback With a 40% Gain Since April 10
author: Anthony Di Pizio
description: The iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV) has rebounded 40% from its April 10 low after Wall Street initially feared AI would disrupt the software industry. Recent earnings reports from major software companies like ServiceNow, Atlassian, and Salesforce show accelerating revenue growth, suggesting AI concerns were overblown. The ETF holds 111 stocks and has historically outperformed the S&P 500 since its 2001 inception.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: ETF has rebounded 40% from its 52-week low and is only 11% below its prior peak. Strong historical performance (9.2% annualized return since 2001) and recent earnings reports from holdings show accelerating revenue growth, suggesting AI concerns were overblown.

Offers cloud-based software applications and serves as an AI infrastructure play with cost-efficient data centers. Positioned to benefit from both enterprise software demand and AI infrastructure buildout.

Offers cloud-based software applications and serves as an AI infrastructure play with cost-efficient data centers. Positioned to benefit from both enterprise software demand and AI infrastructure buildout.
Major enterprise software provider with legacy products (Windows, Microsoft 365) and AI exposure through Copilot and Azure cloud platform. Included in ETF's top holdings.
Leading cybersecurity vendor positioned to benefit from increased AI-driven attacks. Stock trades near record highs and has bucked the software sell-off.
Major cybersecurity vendor expected to experience significant growth as bad actors use AI for more sophisticated attacks. Stock trades near record highs.
Delivered accelerating revenue growth in most recent quarter, contradicting AI disruption thesis. Demonstrates strong enterprise demand despite AI concerns.
Reported accelerating revenue growth and introduced innovative Flex pricing model to adapt to potential AI-driven workforce changes. Author recently bought the stock.
Delivered accelerating revenue growth in most recent quarter, indicating strong enterprise demand and refuting AI disruption concerns.
Cloud observability giant held in the ETF, positioned to benefit from enterprise software infrastructure needs.
